dashboard.title: Lab Overview
dashboard.subtitle: Pipeline · critical-value SLA · AI exception triage
dashboard.tatBanner: {count, plural, one {# test past TAT target} other {# tests past TAT target}}
dashboard.callbackNotifyBody: Lab notified {recipient} of critical result for {patient}. Read-receipt at bedside.
dashboard.callbackLoggedToast: Callback logged for {patient} to {recipient} · SLA closed
dashboard.tatBreachTitle: {count, plural, one {# lab TAT breach} other {# lab TAT breaches}}
dashboard.tatBreachBody: {count, plural, one {Lab has # test past TAT. Pulling on-call lab tech to clear.} other {Lab has # tests past TAT. Pulling on-call lab tech to clear.}}
dashboard.tatEscalatedToast: {count, plural, one {Escalated # TAT breach · admin + doctor notified} other {Escalated # TAT breaches · admin + doctor notified}}

phlebotomy.title: Phlebotomy queue
phlebotomy.subtitle: Call patient · draw samples · barcode + route to bench
phlebotomy.lastSync: Queue auto-syncs when doctor places new orders · last synced {time}
phlebotomy.queueClearHint: Every ordered sample has been drawn. Orders placed by doctors appear here automatically.
phlebotomy.pipelineDownstreamBody: After collection, samples auto-route to their section bench (Biochem · Hema · Immuno · Urine · Micro). Section tech claims → enters values → pathologist verifies → result released to doctor.
phlebotomy.noSpecimens: No specimens defined for this order.
phlebotomy.verifyIdentity: Verify identity (name + DOB), tube colours, and adequate volume before saving.
phlebotomy.tickEveryTube: Tick every tube as drawn before saving.
phlebotomy.sampleCollectedBody: {tubes, plural, one {# tube} other {# tubes}} drawn ({barcode}). {tests, plural, one {# test} other {# tests}} routed to bench.

inbox.title: Lab Inbox
inbox.subtitle: Phlebotomy & sample collection · orders coming in from across the hospital
inbox.emptyAwaiting: Inbox is clear — no orders awaiting collection
inbox.collectedToast: Collected · {tubes, plural, one {# tube} other {# tubes}} barcoded · {tests, plural, one {# test} other {# tests}} routed to benches
inbox.recollectBodyFresh: Lab requesting a fresh draw for {name}.
inbox.specimenRejectedToast: Specimen {accession} rejected · recollect auto-created · doctor + nurse notified

verify.title: Pathologist verification
verify.subtitle: Sign off entered results · second-read with reference ranges + delta-check · release to ordering doctor
verify.aiSecondReadHint: Scan the released panel for out-of-range values; accept to escalate criticals to the ordering doctor.
verify.queueClearHint: All entered results have been verified and released.
verify.verifiedToastBody: Verified and released by {verifier}. {count, plural, one {# abnormal flag} other {# abnormal flags}}.
verify.rejectedToast: {test} rejected — back to entry for re-work
verify.afterReleaseBody: On release, the doctor gets a notification (critical-priority if any CH/CL flag), the patient portal updates, billing logs the test, and the audit row is written.

benches.title: Benches
benches.subtitle: Claim → enter results → send for verification → verify → release · live H/L/Critical flagging against reference ranges
benches.noTests: No tests on this bench right now
benches.queueHint: Accept a specimen to start processing
benches.verifiedByLine: Verified by {name} · ready to release to the ordering doctor.
benches.allAnalyteValuesRequired: All analyte values are required before sending for verification.

microbiology.title: Microbiology
microbiology.subtitle: Multi-day culture · Inoculated → Growth check → Identified → AST → Final · final report releases the test
microbiology.hintInoculated: Fresh cultures land here when their specimen is collected.
microbiology.hintGrowthCheck: Day 1 — check plates for growth or finalise as no-growth.
microbiology.hintIdentified: Day 2 — organism identified; run an antibiotic sensitivity panel next.
microbiology.hintAst: Day 3 — review sensitivities, then write the final report.
microbiology.hintFinal: Finalised reports stay here until the next session.
microbiology.noGrowthReport: No growth after {hours} hours of incubation
microbiology.noSignificantGrowthReport: No significant growth identified — contaminant flora only
microbiology.defaultFinalReport:
Final report — {name} isolated.
AST: {ast}.
Clinical correlation advised.

analyzerFeed.title: Analyzer feed
analyzerFeed.subtitlePart1: Modern lab default — collected samples auto-loaded onto analyzers · results push to LIMS over HL7 · no human types numbers for routine assays.
analyzerFeed.fallbackBody: Need to override a result (dilution, reagent error) or run a manual assay (micro, smear, ESR)? Use
analyzerFeed.fallbackBodyEnd: — section tech can claim, type values, and finish entry just like before.
analyzerFeed.howBodyPart1: Each collected sample sits on its analyzer's queue. Click
analyzerFeed.howBodyPart2: on an analyzer to push every pending result at once, or hit
analyzerFeed.howBodyPart3: on a single row to push just that one. Turn on
analyzerFeed.howBodyPart4: to simulate analyzers ticking through the queue on their own. Results land in the
analyzerFeed.howBodyPart5: queue for pathologist sign-off — same as a real lab.
analyzerFeed.moreQueued: +{count} more queued · use "Process queue" to clear
analyzerFeed.manualFallbackBody: For micro (cultures, AST), urine micro, peripheral smear, ESR, or any time you need to override an auto-fed value (dilution, reagent error, instrument flag), use Manual entries. The section tech claims the test → types each analyte → finishes entry. Pathologist verifies the same way.
analyzerFeed.resultPushed: Result pushed by analyzer · awaiting pathologist
analyzerFeed.batchPushed: {analyzer}: {count, plural, one {# result} other {# results}} pushed

qc.title: Quality Control
qc.subtitle: Levey-Jennings runs · Westgard rules · active violations block release on the affected analyzer

reflex.title: Reflex Tests
reflex.subtitle: AI-suggested follow-up tests triggered automatically when a result trips a rule · one-click to order
reflex.noPendingHint: Release a result on Benches that trips a rule (e.g. high HbA1c, high Troponin, leucocytosis) to populate this list.
